message: Morning, all. Laptop open, brain mostly engaged - what's our angle today?
Could the honey note be doing too much in that second paragraph, or does it actually tie back to the warmth thing you were going for?
The caramelization is the real story - honey's just the vehicle. Let me rebuild that second paragraph around the actual transformation happening in the pan.
The caramelization angle works - that's the real technique people need to understand.
The caramelization rebuild sounds right - maybe lean harder on the browning stages so people know exactly when to pull it off the heat?
Browning stages - that's the detail that matters. Copy's locked. Good work.
